mnemonics: CPU, Memory, and I/O in a Box
keyPoints: Definition of Virtualization: Creating a virtual environment to isolate resources and improve efficiency
Virtualization Structure/Tools and Mechanisms: Hypervisors, Virtual Machines, and Device Drivers
Virtualization of CPU/Memory and I/O devices: Intel VT, AMD-V, and Virtualization-Ready CPUs
Virtual Clusters and Resource Management: Virtualization Administration, Clustering, and Load Balancing
Benefits of Virtualization: Increased Agility, Improved Resource Utilization, and Enhanced Security
